findings.description: StreamingMedian.add uses bisect.insort, whose list insertion shifts all trailing elements at O(n) per call, making building a stream of N values O(n^2) despite the class advertising a 'running/streaming' median that a two-heap (heapq) structure would serve in O(log n) per insert.

safe_mean materializes the full iterable into a list when a single-pass running sum plus count would compute the same result in O(1) extra space, but for typical dashboard-sized inputs the memory cost is negligible.

percentile performs a full O(n log n) sort for a single percentile query; if many percentiles of one dataset were needed a single shared sort or O(n) selection (quickselect) would be cheaper, but for one-shot calls the full sort is inherent and fine.
